title: Introduction to SEQuential
text: assumptions must met avoid unintended errors using SEQuential. : User provided time.col begins 0 per unique id.col entries, also assume column contains integers continues 1 every time step. e.g. (0, 1, 2, 3, …) allowed (0, 1, 2, 2.5, …) (0, 1, 2, 4, 5, …) . Provided time.col entries may order sort enforced beginning function, e.g. (0, 2, 1, 4, 3, …) valid begins 0 continuously increasing increments 1, even though ordered. eligible column names provided excused.cols binary (0/1) flag variables (respect time.col)

title: Introduction to SEQuential
text: R script, always start defining options object, SEQopts() helper. many defaults allow target exactly like change analysis. wiki specific pages dedicated causal contrast parameters affect , simplicity let’s start intention--treat analysis 20 bootstrap samples. general, options form {option}.{parameter} - may notice use bootstrap.nboot indicating parameter affects bootstrap
code: library(SEQTaRget)  options <- SEQopts(km.curves = TRUE, #asks the function to return survival and risk estimates                    bootstrap = TRUE, #asks the model to perform bootstrapping                    bootstrap.nboot = 20) #asks the model for 20 bootstrap samples

title: Introduction to SEQuential
text: next step running primary R function, SEQuential(). give options, data, data-level information. provide small simulated datasets test . provide print statements help track SEQuential() function processing given point time.

title: Parameter Builder for SEQuential Model and Estimates — SEQopts
text: bootstrap Logical: defines SEQuential() run bootstrapping, default FALSE bootstrap.nboot Integer: number bootstraps, default 100 bootstrap.sample Numeric: percentage data use bootstrapping, [0, 1], default 0.8 bootstrap.CI Numeric: defines confidence interval bootstrapping, default 0.95 (95% CI) bootstrap.CI_method Character: selects way calculate bootstraps confidence intervals ("se", "percentile"), default "se" cense String: column name additional censoring variable, e.g. loss--follow-cense.denominator String: censoring denominator covariates right hand side formula object cense.eligible String: column name indicator column defining rows use censoring model cense.numerator String: censoring numerator covariates right hand side formula object compevent String: column name competing event indicator covariates String: covariates right hand side formula object data.return Logical: whether return expanded dataframe weighting information, default FALSE denominator String character vector: denominator covariates right hand side formula object. single string fits model every treatment arm. vector one formula per treat.level (treat.level order) fits separate denominator model, covariates, arm; supported post-expansion weights (weight.preexpansion = FALSE) deviation Logical: create switch based deviation column deviation.col, default FALSE deviation.col Character: column name deviation deviation.conditions Character list: RHS evaluations length treat.levels deviation.excused Logical: whether deviations excused deviation.excused_cols, default FALSE deviation.excused_cols Character list: excused columns deviation switches excused Logical: case censoring, whether excused condition, default FALSE excused.cols List: list column names treatment switch excuses - length, ordered treat.level expand.Logical: TRUE, SEQuential() returns expanded data.table immediately expansion skips weighting, outcome modelling survival/risk steps. Useful need expanded dataset (e.g. inspect store separately). Default FALSE fastglm.method Integer: decomposition method fastglm (0L-column-pivoted QR, 1L-unpivoted QR, 2L-LLT Cholesky, 3L-LDLT Cholesky, 4L-full-pivoted QR, 5L-Bidiagonal Divide Conquer SVD), default 2L followup.class Logical: treat followup class, e.g. expands every time indicator column, default FALSE followup.include Logical: whether include 'followup' 'followup_squared' outcome model, default TRUE followup.max Numeric: maximum time expand , default Inf (maximum) followup.min Numeric: minimum follow-time since trial enrollment include, must non-negative, default 0 followup.spline Logical: treat followup natural cubic spline (splines::ns()), default FALSE followup.spline.df Integer: degrees freedom passed splines::ns() followup.spline = TRUE. df = k, ns() places k - 1 interior knots quantiles followup. Must >= 1; df = 1 equivalent linear term generally want. Default 4 (3 interior knots). glm.package Character: package use fitting GLMs, either "fastglm" (default) "parglm". "parglm" selected nthreads option controls number threads passed parglm::parglm.fit(). realistic SEQTaRget workloads (expanded datasets approximately million rows) "fastglm" faster; "parglm" may help substantially larger datasets parallel chunking outweighs setup overhead. Note bootstrap = TRUE main fit uses "parglm": bootstrap refits always use "fastglm", warm-started main fit's coefficients, faster per resample parglm's per-fit thread setup. hazard Logical: hazard error calculation instead survival estimation, default FALSE indicator.baseline String: identifier baseline variables covariates, numerator, denominator - intended override indicator.squared String: identifier squared variables covariates, numerator, denominator - intended override km.curves Logical: Kaplan-Meier survival curve creation data return, default FALSE multinomial Logical: whether expect multilevel treatment values, default FALSE ncores Integer: number cores use parallel processing, default one less system max, see parallelly::availableCores() nthreads Integer: number threads use data.table processing, default data.table::getDTthreads() numerator String character vector: numerator covariates right hand side formula object. single string fits model every treatment arm. vector one formula per treat.level (treat.level order) fits separate numerator model, covariates, arm; supported post-expansion weights (weight.preexpansion = FALSE) parallel Logical: define SEQuential process run parallel, default FALSE parglm.control control object parglm::parglm.control() pass parglm::parglm.fit(). used glm.package = "parglm". Defaults parglm::parglm.control(method = "FAST"). encounter chol(): decomposition failed error (e.g. near-singular model matrices large datasets), pass parglm.control = parglm::parglm.control(method = "LAPACK") use numerically stable QR decomposition instead, switch using fastglm backend. plot.colors Character: Colors output plot km.curves = TRUE, defaulted ggplot2 defaults plot.labels Character: Color labels output plot km.curves = TRUE order e.g. c("risk.0", "risk.1") plot.subtitle Character: Subtitle output plot km.curves = TRUE plot.title Character: Title output plot km.curves = TRUE plot.type Character: Type plot create km.curves = TRUE, available options 'survival' (default), 'risk', 'inc' (case censoring) risk.times Numeric vector: follow-times (data's follow-units) report risk difference risk ratio km.curves = TRUE. requested time snapped latest available follow-. final follow-time always included; follow-grid zero-indexed (followup runs 0:survival.max), final time survival.max + 1, e.g. survival.max = 120 reports row 121. Default NA reports final follow-time. seed Integer: starting seed; default NULL draws single random integer SEQopts() called, set explicitly reproducible bootstrap results selection.first_trial Logical: selects first eligible trial expanded dataset, default FALSE selection.prob Numeric: percent total IDs select selection.random, bound [0, 1], default 0.8 selection.random Logical: randomly selects IDs replacement run analysis, default FALSE subgroup Character: Column name stratify outcome models survival.max Numeric: maximum time survival curves, default Inf (maximum) treat.level List: treatment levels compare, default c(0, 1) trial.include Logical: whether include 'trial' 'trial_squared' outcome model, default TRUE visit String: column name visit indicator variable, e.g. "visit" visit.denominator String: visit denominator covariates right hand side formula object visit.numerator String: visit numerator covariates right hand side formula object weight.eligible_cols List: list column names indicator columns defining weights eligible weight models - order treat.level weight.lower Numeric: IPCW weights truncated lower bound, must non-negative, default 0. Truncation applied weights used fit outcome model; weights reported weight.statistics returned data (data.return = TRUE) untruncated values. weight.lag_condition Logical: whether weights conditioned treatment lag value, default TRUE weight.p99 Logical: forces weight truncation 1st 99th percentile weights, override provided weight.upper weight.lower. percentiles taken untruncated weight distribution (reported weight.statistics), weight.lower/weight.upper truncation affects weights used fit outcome model. weight.preexpansion Logical: whether weighting done pre-expanded data, default TRUE weight.upper Numeric: weights truncated upper end weight, default Inf. weight.lower, truncation affects weights used fit outcome model, reported weight.statistics returned data. weighted Logical: whether perform weighted analysis, default FALSE

text: CRAN release: 2026-06-23 Fix “Risk Differerence” typo risk.times output Report follow-per treatment arm @info slot info$followup.unique info$followup.nonunique (per subgroup, mirroring info$outcome.unique / info$outcome.nonunique). grouped baseline treatment expanded-data rows observed (non-NA) outcome - person-time outcome model fit . non-unique table counts follow-intervals (non-unique outcome counts divided give per-arm event rates); unique table counts distinct subjects contributing follow-arm. Also shown printed diagnostic tables. Speed hazard ratio calculation fitting Cox model survival C fitters directly prebuilt design matrix instead coxph(formula, data), avoiding model.frame/model.matrix rebuild every bootstrap iteration: survival::coxph.fit() non-competing-event model survival::agreg.fit() competing-event Fine-Gray (counting-process) model. hazard ratio CIs unchanged. Fix competing-event Fine-Gray hazard fit use finegray() case weights (fgwt), required valid subdistribution-hazard estimate previously omitted. -op current hazard simulation (administrative censoring, fgwt 1) corrects estimate simulated data ever carry random censoring. Report competing events per treatment arm @info slot info$compevent.unique info$compevent.nonunique, mirroring structure info$outcome.unique / info$outcome.nonunique. grouped baseline treatment; non-unique table counts competing event occurrences expanded data unique table counts distinct subjects experienced competing event. NA compevent specified. SEQuential() fit, populate weight.statistics outcome.model hazard = TRUE. Warn numerator denominator weight models given identical covariates. case stabilized weights equal 1 (.e., weighting), usually typo denominator argument. Improve SEQuential() helpfile adding per-protocol example. Add behavioural tests selection.random = TRUE retains treated trial-starts, subsamples control trial-starts requested selection.prob fraction, reproducible fixed seed; rename previous smoke test actually exercise feature. Add behavioural tests ; weight.lower/weight.upper truncation, weight.p99 truncation, followup.include/trial.include, followup.class, weight.lag_condition, followup.min/followup.max, weight.eligible_cols. Fix numerator() denominator() returning NULL every weighted model; now return fitted per-arm numerator/denominator weight models. Document weight truncation applies outcome-model fit. Pass formula cache inline.pred() weight models Fix SEQOpts() argument ordering. Bump codecov/codecov-action v7 Report censored/uncensored split verbose expansion output Fix params@data divergence expansion uses checked repaired data Fix --one bootstrap model pairing internal.hazard() Fix serial bootstrap seed internal.survival() replicate standardizes resample outcome model fit Fix “incorrect number dimensions” error user column shares name internal data.table variable (e.g. outcome column named ), hoisting row-index computations DT[] expressions columns shadow local variables Fix fastglm.method validation accept fastglm’s full range 0-5 (0, column-pivoted QR, fastglm’s default previously rejected; 4 full-pivoted QR 5 Bidiagonal Divide Conquer SVD; six supported since fastglm 0.0.1) update documentation accordingly Fix default seed unseeded runs genuinely random. Previously full .Random.seed vector stored set.seed() silently used first element - RNG kind code, constant - every unseeded run drew identical bootstrap resamples. default now single random integer drawn SEQopts() called. Fix spurious “Maximum followup survival curves” warning followup.max set survival.max left Inf default. check now runs Inf defaults resolved, also catches previously missed case finite survival.max exceeding data-derived followup.max. Fix show() erroring SEQoutput object subgroup compevent specified (competing event section passed model list rather subgroup name cat()); empty competing event model entries now also skipped instead printing NULL. Fix silent merging bootstrap copies large numeric subject IDs. arithmetic relabeling (orig_id * multiplier + copy index) exceeds 2^53 exact-integer range doubles roughly 8+ digit IDs, consecutive copy indices round value distinct copies subject collapse together -ID grouping (corrupting cumulative-product weights). Relabeling now falls back string concatenation whenever arithmetic overflow, IDs negative non-integer. Fix hazard bootstrap dropping resampling multiplicity. hazard bootstrap sampler relabel bootstrap copies, identical copies subject drawn collapsed simulation’s -(id, trial) grouping, making replicate behave like subsample rather bootstrap understating hazard ratio CI width. Copies now relabeled uniquely; point estimates unchanged. Fix hardcoded "_sq" suffix pre-expansion weight data custom indicator.squared longer fails “column found” weight models. Fix method = "dose-response" erroring custom indicator.squared. expansion created dose_sq/trial_sq hardcoded names (excluded literal dose_sq expansion variables) default covariates referenced paste0("dose", indicator.squared), non-default indicator failed SEQexpand(). internally generated squared columns (dose, trial, expanded data survival prediction grid) now follow indicator.squared, matching existing convention followup, trial time. Remove dead trialID construction survival-curve standardization. per-row paste0() label built full standardization population (every bootstrap resample) never read: predictions row-wise -ID grouping, bootstrap multiplicity carried duplicated rows . Results unchanged. Vectorise survival-curve CI clamping pmax()/pmin() instead evaluating scalar max()/min() per row via = .. Results unchanged. Narrow per-iteration copy internal.weights() columns weight models actually use (ids, structure, treatment baseline copy, formula covariates, censoring/visit/eligibility indicators, excused flags) instead copying every column expanded (post-expansion weighting) input (pre-expansion weighting) table every bootstrap iteration. Results unchanged. Drop unmatched rows time-varying covariate join SEQexpand() nomatch = NULL (remove -op .SDcols ). Original-data rows expansion-grid match - possible followup.min > 0 selection.random - carried NA-trial rows squared-column computation discarded subsequent inner join baseline table. Results unchanged. Replace seq.int(1:.N) seq_len(.N) hazard simulation’s per-trial follow-construction, avoiding double allocation per (id, trial) group; column now integer, matching expansion’s convention. Results unchanged. Document glm.package = "parglm" bootstrap = TRUE main fit uses parglm: bootstrap refits always use fastglm, warm-started main fit’s coefficients, faster per resample parglm’s per-fit thread setup. previously silent switch. Clarify unique vs non-unique diagnostic table labels docs Soft deprecate SEQestimate() since accurate order magnitude. Add bootstrap standard errors risk.comparison output: RD SE (standard error risk difference, natural scale) log(RR) SE (standard error log risk ratio). reported whenever bootstrap = TRUE, regardless bootstrap.CI_method (SEs already computed internally form "se" confidence intervals retained, computed "percentile"). Fix confidence-interval column labels risk.comparison risk.data, hardcoded 95% regardless bootstrap.CI. now reflect requested level (e.g. RD 90% LCI, 90% UCI bootstrap.CI = 0.9).

title: SEQTaRget v1.4.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-21 Remove mention units time docs. Improve memory usage bootstrapping. Fix --one labeling survival output followup = k correctly represents survival k intervals, adding row followup = survival.max + 1 final interval’s estimate. Fix expansion bug subjects experiencing outcome early incorrectly carried forward outcome=0 rows subsequent periods truncating trial first event row (thanks, @francescazaccagnino) Add expand.option SEQopts(). TRUE, SEQuential() returns expanded data.table directly skips analysis steps, users want inspect store expanded dataset . Fix followup.spline = TRUE basis genuinely non-linear. Splines now built model formula via splines::ns() instead applied single-column transform followup, new followup.spline.df option (default 4) controls number basis functions. treatment--followup interaction now uses spline basis. Knots baked full expanded followup fit time basis identical fit prediction time across bootstraps survival grids. Internally, formula column extraction now uses .vars(), user-supplied covariates may include ns(), bs(), (), factor(), poly() etc. without breaking expansion. Rename format.time() format_time() wasn’t S3 method hence causing roxygen2 write incorrect information helpfile. Add package level helpfile bump roxygen2 8.0.0. Add parglm alternative GLM fitting backend. Add warm starts bootstrap GLM fits. Add dataset size summary verbose output. Fix selection.random propagated SEQopts() internal parameters. Cap data.table 2 threads tests vignette builds, skip multisession parallel test CRAN, comply CRAN’s 2-core policy checks. Apply SEQopts(nthreads = ...) setting data.table SEQuential(). Previously used parglm backend ignored default serial fastglm path, data.table ran global default thread count. previous global setting restored call finishes. Add risk.times option SEQopts(). km.curves = TRUE, risk difference risk ratio (CIs) reported requested follow-time, just end follow-. Requested times snapped latest available follow-, final time always included. risk.comparison risk.data tables gain Followup column. Fix factorize() also coerce categorical (character) time-varying covariates - baseline (_bas) counterparts - factors levels fixed full data. Previously fixed treatment columns factorized, character time-varying covariate realise different level sets across bootstrap resamples raise “newdata provided match fitted model” (often bootstrapped hazard analyses larger samples smaller bootstrap.sample). Numeric time-varying covariates left unchanged.
